Girls Trip (2017)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $115,108,515Details
International Box Office $25,777,838
Worldwide Box Office $140,886,353
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $8,355,345
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $4,911,642
Total $13,266,987
Further financial details...

Synopsis

When four lifelong friends travel to New Orleans for the annual Essence Festival, sisterhoods are rekindled, wild sides are rediscovered, and there’s enough dancing, drinking, brawling and romancing to make the Big Easy blush.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$31,201,920 (27.1% of total gross)
Legs:3.69 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:81.7% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$28,000,000 (worldwide box office is 5.0 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,591 opening theaters/2,648 max. theaters, 6.8 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $153,478,016

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: July 21st, 2017 (Wide) by Universal
International Releases: July 20th, 2017 (Wide) (Panama)
July 20th, 2017 (Wide) (Central America)
July 21st, 2017 (Wide) (Aruba)
July 28th, 2017 (Wide) (United Kingdom)
August 4th, 2017 (Wide) (South Africa)
Video Release: October 3rd, 2017 by Universal Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: R for crude and sexual content throughout, pervasive language, brief graphic nudity, and drug material.
(Rating bulletin 2460 (Cert #50865), 1/25/2017)
Running Time: 122 minutes
Franchise: Girls Trip
Geographical setting: Louisiana, New Orleans, LA
Plot point: African Americans, Road Trip
Social setting: Girls' Night Out, Music Festival
Subgenre: Buddy Comedy
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Comedy
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Will Packer Productions, Universal Pictures, Perfect World Pictures
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

When four lifelong friends travel to New Orleans for the annual Essence Festival, sisterhoods are rekindled, wild sides are rediscovered, and there’s enough dancing, drinking, brawling and romancing to make the Big Easy blush.
